data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0015/sessions/0022-horizon-T14

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# Orientation for the next Horizon agent

- Useful context: the Hilbert lane now bridges Serre finiteness to the sorry-free rationality engine. `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SerreFiniteness.lean` carries `GradedHilbert` (engine-input packaging), the axiom-clean `GradedHilbert.isRatHilb` (calls `gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational` in `GradedHilbertSerre.lean`), and the glue `hilbertFunction_isRatHilb` / `hilbertPolynomial_eval_eventually_of_projective`. Blueprint: `subsec:graded_hilbert_bridge` in `Picard_QuotScheme.tex` (all `\uses` resolve; `lem:gradedHilbert_fiber` statement `\leanok`, proof intentionally not).

- Relevant files for the one open leaf: `SerreFiniteness.lean` has exactly two named-leaf `sorry`s — `sectionGradedModule_fg:66` (abstract Serre, still consumed by `sectionGradedModule_fg_fiber`/`hilbertFunction_finiteDimensional`) and the new companion `gradedHilbert_fiber:262` (adds the degree-1 coordinate action from `X_s ↪ ℙ^d`). Both need ℙⁿ coherent-sheaf cohomology absent from Mathlib v4.31 (no `Serre`/`IsAmple`/`VeryAmple`/Proj-cohomology). Inbox `I-0109` tracks the lane; item 2 (this bridge) is now built, items 1 (deep leaf) and 3 (`QuotFunctor` consuming `hilbertPolynomial`) remain.

- Useful context for downstream: `hilbertPolynomial_eval_eventually_of_projective` / `hilbertFunction_isRatHilb` are consumable as leaf-conditional inputs for the QuotScheme restatement (`I-0118`, `AJC.picrep` cone) without touching the deep leaf. `quot_boundedness` (Castelnuovo–Mumford) is deliberately blueprint-only — a faithful Lean statement needs `Rⁱπ_*`-of-twists vocabulary that does not exist (`I-0118` trap genus).

- Environment: `Scheme.Modules.glue` is universe-monomorphic (`Scheme.{0}`), so `serreTwist`, `IsProjectiveWith`, and the `GradedHilbert`/fibre machinery live at `Scheme.{0}`; a QuotScheme restatement specialises there unless the glue engine is generalised (`I-0126`). Full `lake build` was green (8666 jobs) at commit `3063f29ffd`; working tree clean.

# Task focus
Task: T14
Title: Ampleness / projective-morphism / Serre-finiteness foundation (unblocks Picard representability)
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/*.lean,MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex

Build the ample / very-ample line-bundle and projective-morphism foundation that Mathlib v4.31 lacks. This is the SINGLE shared prerequisite blocking QuotScheme representability, curve projectivity, and the Picard-scheme root instHasPicScheme (see inbox I-0109 ampleness gap and I-0118 statement trap). It has NO prerequisite and unblocks T15. Do NOT touch the FGA assembly or attempt QuotScheme representability here — build only the foundation.

MATHLIB v4.31 ASSETS TO BUILD ON (verified present): AlgebraicGeometry.Proj (ProjectiveSpectrum/Scheme.lean); Proj.toSpecZero with instance IsProper for finite-type graded algebras (ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean:368), LocallyOfFiniteType (:136), IsSeparated (:82); Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:439) and Proj.map functoriality (Functor.lean:144); MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule / MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra (RingTheory/MvPolynomial/Homogeneous.lean:608); AffineSpace pattern (AlgebraicGeometry/AffineSpace.lean:44); SheafOfModules.unit. Verified ABSENT (must be built): any IsProjective/QuasiProjective morphism class, VeryAmple/IsAmple for sheaves, relative Proj / P^n_S over a base, Serre twisting sheaf O(1).

DELIVERABLES (new sorry-free Lean files or honestly named-leaf-factored, each with a complete-proof blueprint node):
1. Relative projective space P(n;S) := pullback (terminal.from S) (terminal.from (Proj (MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin (n+1)) Int))), in the AffineSpace.lean:44 style; derive proper + locallyOfFiniteType + separated structural morphism by base change from the Mathlib Proj instances.
2. Serre twist O(1) (and O(m)) as a SheafOfModules on Proj 𝒜, glued over the awayι / basic-open affine cover with transition t_i/t_j — REUSE the project module-gluing engine (Picard/GlueDescent.lean, GrassmannianZariskiSheaf.lean, Scheme.Modules.glue).
3. A projective-with-L predicate: pi : X ⟶ S is projective carrying L when there is a closed immersion i : X ↪ P(n;S) over S with L ≅ i^* O(1). Prove stability facts the endgame consumes: base change, composition with closed immersions, and that it implies IsProper.
4. Serre finiteness / boundedness: for pi proper with L relatively ample, the section graded module ⊕_m Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^⊗m) is finitely generated over ⊕_m Γ(X_s, L_s^⊗m) (blueprint lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, I-0109, the deep remaining Hilbert-lane input) and the uniform Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity bound (blueprint lem:quot_boundedness). The DirectSum-decomposition bridge to gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational (already sorry-free) is documented in I-0109.

Verify each file with lake build (or lake env lean) + lean_verify axiom checks. Prefer axiom-clean; where a step is genuinely too deep, factor a NAMED typed sorry with an honest statement + blueprint node. Coordinate blueprint nodes with the existing Picard_QuotScheme.tex / Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex chapters.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [done/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk…

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

data.content:
1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.ProjectiveMorphism
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.HilbertPolynomial
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.PullbackFinitePresentation
10	
11	/-!
12	# Serre finiteness for the section graded module (statement)
13	
14	The deep remaining input of the Hilbert-polynomial lane
15	(`lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`, inbox `I-0109`): for a projective scheme over
16	a field carrying a very ample line bundle `L` and a coherent sheaf `F`, the
17	section graded ring `R(X, L) = ⊕ₘ Γ(X, L^{⊗m})` is Noetherian, the section
18	graded module `M(X, F, L) = ⊕ₘ Γ(X, F ⊗ L^{⊗m})` is a finite module over it,
19	and each graded component is a finite-dimensional `κ`-vector space.
20	
21	This file gives the **honest statement** against the projectivity vocabulary
22	of `Picard/ProjectiveMorphism.lean` (the `IsProjectiveWith` closed-immersion
23	predicate — very ampleness in the encoding settled in inbox `I-0118`), as a
24	named typed `sorry` leaf: the proof is Serre's theorem ([Hartshorne] II.5.17
25	ff., [Nitsure] §1) and is genuinely deep (multi-session; it needs the
26	pushforward-to-projective-space reduction and cohomological finiteness on
27	`ℙⁿ`).
28	
29	Compared with the blueprint node, which hypothesises an *ample* `L` and
30	passes to a Veronese power, the Lean statement hypothesises the very ample
31	form directly (`IsProjectiveWith`) — this is the form in which the
32	Quot-scheme endgame ([Nitsure] §5) consumes it.
33	
34	Blueprint: `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`
35	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
36	-/
37	
38	open CategoryTheory Limits
39	
40	noncomputable section
41	
42	universe u
43	
44	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
45	
46	/-- The structural `κ`-algebra structure on the global sections of a scheme
47	over `Spec κ` (the absolute analogue of `Scheme.Hom.fiberResidueMap`). -/
48	def Scheme.Hom.structuralSectionsHom {X : Scheme.{0}} {κ : Type} [CommRing κ]
49	    (π : X ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of κ)) : κ →+* Γ(X, ⊤) :=
50	  ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of κ)).inv ≫ π.appTop).hom
51	
52	/-- **Serre finiteness for the section graded module**
53	(`lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`; [Nitsure] §1, [Hartshorne] II.5.17 ff.):
54	for `π : X ⟶ Spec κ` projective carrying the very ample line bundle `L`
55	and a coherent (finitely presented) sheaf `F` on `X`,
56	
57	1. the section graded ring `⊕ₘ Γ(X, L^{⊗m})` is Noetherian;
58	2. the section graded module `⊕ₘ Γ(X, F ⊗ L^{⊗m})` is a finite module
59	   over it;
60	3. each component `Γ(X, F ⊗ L^{⊗m})` is a finite-dimensional `κ`-vector
61	   space (via the structural `κ`-action).
62	
63	NAMED LEAF (`sorry`): the geometric heart of the Hilbert-polynomial lane
64	(inbox `I-0109`), awaiting the pushforward-to-`ℙⁿ` reduction and
65	cohomological finiteness of coherent sheaves on projective space. -/
66	theorem sectionGradedModule_fg {X : Scheme.{0}} {κ : Type} [Field κ]
67	    (π : X ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of κ)) (L F : X.Modules)
68	    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
69	    (letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedRing_gsemiring L
70	     IsNoetherianRing (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.sectionDeg L)))
71	    ∧ (letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedRing_gsemiring L
72	       letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedModule_gmodule F L
73	       Module.Finite (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.sectionDeg L))
74	         (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg F L)))
75	    ∧ ∀ m : ℕ,
76	        letI := Module.compHom (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg F L m)
77	          π.structuralSectionsHom
78	        FiniteDimensional κ (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg F L m) := by
79	  sorry
80	
81	/-! ## Descent to scheme-theoretic fibres — the Hilbert-lane connector
82	
83	The absolute leaf `sectionGradedModule_fg` is stated over a *field*; the Hilbert
84	polynomial `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` and its graded Hilbert function
85	`Scheme.hilbertFunction` (`Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean`) live at the
86	scheme-theoretic fibre `X_s = π.fiber s` over the residue field `κ(s)`, which is
87	a field.  The lemmas below are the sorry-free geometric glue that transports
88	`IsProjectiveWith` and finite presentation to a fibre (both via the already
89	proved `IsProjectiveWith.baseChange` and `Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation`),
90	so the deep Serre leaf applies fibrewise; the corollary
91	`hilbertFunction_finiteDimensional` then shows the graded Hilbert function is a
92	genuine (finite) dimension for projective fibres — the finiteness half of the
93	bridge to `AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`
94	(inbox `I-0109`).  The remaining step — packaging finite generation as the
95	`MvPolynomial`-finiteness input of that engine — is documented there. -/
96	
97	variable {X S : Scheme.{0}}
98	
99	/-- **`IsProjectiveWith` descends to scheme-theoretic fibres**: if `π : X ⟶ S`
100	is projective carrying `L`, then the structural morphism of the fibre
101	`X_s = π.fiber s`, namely `π.fiberToSpecResidueField s : X_s ⟶ Spec κ(s)`, is
102	projective carrying the restriction `L_s = π.fiberModule s L`.  Immediate from
103	`IsProjectiveWith.baseChange` along `S.fromSpecResidueField s`, since the fibre
104	is the base change of `π` along that morphism (`Scheme.Hom.fiber`). -/
105	theorem Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.fiber {π : X ⟶ S} {L : X.Modules}
106	    (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (s : S) :
107	    (π.fiberToSpecResidueField s).IsProjectiveWith (π.fiberModule s L) :=
108	  h.baseChange (S.fromSpecResidueField s)
109	
110	/-- **Finite presentation descends to scheme-theoretic fibres**: the restriction
111	`F_s = π.fiberModule s F` of a finitely presented sheaf `F` to the fibre is
112	finitely presented (pullback of a finitely presented sheaf along the fibre
113	embedding `π.fiberι s`). -/
114	theorem Scheme.Hom.fiberModule_isFinitePresentation (π : X ⟶ S) (s : S)
115	    {F : X.Modules} (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
116	    (π.fiberModule s F).IsFinitePresentation :=
117	  Scheme.Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation (π.fiberι s) F hF
118	
119	/-- **Serre finiteness along a fibre** (`lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` specialised
120	to the fibre over `s`): for `π : X ⟶ S` projective carrying `L` and `F` finitely
121	presented, the section graded ring of the fibre restriction `L_s` is Noetherian,
122	and the section graded module of `F_s` is finite over it.  This is the deep leaf
123	`sectionGradedModule_fg` applied to the fibre morphism via
124	`IsProjectiveWith.fiber` and `fiberModule_isFinitePresentation`; it inherits the
125	single named `sorry` of that leaf and no other.  The finite-dimensionality of
126	each graded component — the third conclusion of `sectionGradedModule_fg`,
127	matching the graded Hilbert function — is recorded separately as
128	`hilbertFunction_finiteDimensional` (stated with the `Scheme.hilbertFunction`
129	residue-field module structure). -/
130	theorem sectionGradedModule_fg_fiber (π : X ⟶ S) (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)
131	    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
132	    (letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedRing_gsemiring (π.fiberModule s L)
133	     IsNoetherianRing (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.sectionDeg (π.fiberModule s L))))
134	    ∧ (letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedRing_gsemiring (π.fiberModule s L)
135	       letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedModule_gmodule
136	         (π.fiberModule s F) (π.fiberModule s L)
137	       Module.Finite (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.sectionDeg (π.fiberModule s L)))
138	         (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg
139	           (π.fiberModule s F) (π.fiberModule s L)))) :=
140	  ⟨(sectionGradedModule_fg (π.fiberToSpecResidueField s) (π.fiberModule s L)
141	      (π.fiberModule s F) (hproj.fiber s)
142	      (Scheme.Hom.fiberModule_isFinitePresentation π s hF)).1,
143	   (sectionGradedModule_fg (π.fiberToSpecResidueField s) (π.fiberModule s L)
144	      (π.fiberModule s F) (hproj.fiber s)
145	      (Scheme.Hom.fiberModule_isFinitePresentation π s hF)).2.1⟩
146	
147	/-- **The graded Hilbert function is a genuine (finite) dimension for projective
148	fibres**: for `π : X ⟶ S` projective carrying `L` and `F` finitely presented,
149	each twisted-section space `Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` whose `κ(s)`-dimension is
150	`Scheme.hilbertFunction π L F s m` is finite-dimensional — so the graded Hilbert
151	function is not merely the `Module.finrank` junk value.  This is the
152	finite-dimensionality half of the input to the graded Hilbert–Serre rationality
153	engine (`AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`, inbox
154	`I-0109`). -/
155	theorem hilbertFunction_finiteDimensional (π : X ⟶ S) (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)
156	    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) (m : ℕ) :
157	    letI := π.fiberSectionsModule s
158	      (Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow (π.fiberModule s F) (π.fiberModule s L) m)
159	    FiniteDimensional (S.residueField s)
160	      Γ(Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow (π.fiberModule s F) (π.fiberModule s L) m, ⊤) :=
161	  (sectionGradedModule_fg (π.fiberToSpecResidueField s) (π.fiberModule s L)
162	    (π.fiberModule s F) (hproj.fiber s)
163	    (Scheme.Hom.fiberModule_isFinitePresentation π s hF)).2.2 m
164	
165	/-! ## Engine-ready packaging and the rationality of the Hilbert function
166	
167	The graded Hilbert–Serre rationality engine
168	`AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`
169	(`Picard/GradedHilbertSerre.lean`, sorry-free) turns an abstract finitely
170	generated `ℕ`-graded `κ`-vector space — one carrying `r` commuting degree-one
171	endomorphisms and finite over the polynomial ring `κ[X₀,…,X_{r-1}]` — into a
172	rational Hilbert function (`AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb`).  To feed the geometric
173	fibre section module `⊕ₘ Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` into that engine we package its
174	input as the structure `GradedHilbert` and factor Serre finiteness **in
175	engine-ready form** as the single honest leaf `gradedHilbert_fiber` (a companion
176	to `sectionGradedModule_fg`: it additionally records the degree-one coordinate
177	action from the very-ample embedding `X_s ↪ ℙ^d`, the `MvPolynomial` reshaping of
178	inbox `I-0109`).  The rationality of the graded Hilbert function
179	`Scheme.hilbertFunction`, and hence the genuineness of the Hilbert polynomial
180	`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (`hilbertPolynomial_eval_eventually_of_projective`),
181	then follow as **sorry-free glue** — the `DirectSum`-decomposition bridge of
182	inbox `I-0109`. -/
183	
184	/-- **Engine-ready packaging of Serre finiteness** for a Hilbert function
185	`f : ℕ → ℕ` over a field `κ`: a finitely generated (over a polynomial ring in `r`
186	degree-one variables) `ℕ`-graded `κ`-vector space whose `n`-th graded piece has
187	dimension `f n`.  This bundles exactly the data and hypotheses consumed by the
188	graded Hilbert–Serre rationality engine
189	`AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`; the engine call is
190	`GradedHilbert.isRatHilb`.  Geometrically `M` is the fibre section module
191	`⊕ₘ Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`, `grading n` is its `n`-th piece, and `gen` are the
192	`d+1` multiplications by the homogeneous coordinate sections. -/
193	structure GradedHilbert (κ : Type u) [Field κ] (f : ℕ → ℕ) where
194	  /-- Number of degree-one polynomial generators (`d+1` homogeneous coordinates). -/
195	  r : ℕ
196	  /-- The ambient graded `κ`-vector space. -/
197	  M : Type u
198	  [addCommGroup : AddCommGroup M]
199	  [module : Module κ M]
200	  /-- The internal `ℕ`-grading. -/
201	  grading : ℕ → Submodule κ M
202	  [decomposition : DirectSum.Decomposition grading]
203	  /-- Each graded piece is finite-dimensional. -/
204	  finiteDimensional : ∀ n, FiniteDimensional κ (grading n)
205	  /-- The `r` commuting degree-one endomorphisms (coordinate multiplications). -/
206	  gen : Fin r → Module.End κ M
207	  /-- The generators commute. -/
208	  gen_comm : ∀ i j, Commute (gen i) (gen j)
209	  /-- Each generator raises the grading degree by one. -/
210	  gen_raisesDegree : ∀ i, GradedModule.RaisesDegree grading (gen i)
211	  /-- The graded pieces realise the prescribed dimensions `f`. -/
212	  finrank_grading : ∀ n, Module.finrank κ (grading n) = f n
213	  /-- `M` is finite over the polynomial ring `κ[X₀,…,X_{r-1}]` acting via `gen`. -/
214	  finite : letI := GradedModule.polyModule gen gen_comm
215	    Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) M
216	
217	-- Register the carried instances as the *projection* instances of the structure, so
218	-- typeclass search returns exactly the terms appearing in `GradedHilbert.finite`'s type
219	-- (avoids the `polyModule`/`compHom` `whnf` blow-up that fresh `haveI` fvars would cause).
220	attribute [instance] GradedHilbert.addCommGroup GradedHilbert.module
221	  GradedHilbert.decomposition GradedHilbert.finiteDimensional
222	
223	/-- The Hilbert function packaged by a `GradedHilbert` datum is a rational Hilbert
224	function of order `r`: the sorry-free graded Hilbert–Serre engine call
225	(`AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`) followed by the
226	dimension identification `GradedHilbert.finrank_grading`. -/
227	theorem GradedHilbert.isRatHilb {κ : Type u} [Field κ] {f : ℕ → ℕ}
228	    (H : GradedHilbert κ f) : IsRatHilb (fun n => (f n : ℚ)) H.r := by
229	  -- The carried instances are found via the structure's projection instances
230	  -- (registered above), matching the terms in `H.finite`'s type exactly, so the
231	  -- finiteness argument unifies without a `polyModule`/`compHom` `whnf` blow-up.
232	  have h : IsRatHilb (fun n => (Module.finrank κ (H.grading n) : ℚ)) H.r :=
233	    gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational H.grading H.gen H.gen_comm
234	      H.gen_raisesDegree H.finite
235	  have hfun : (fun n => (f n : ℚ))
236	      = fun n => (Module.finrank κ (H.grading n) : ℚ) := by
237	    funext n; rw [H.finrank_grading]
238	  rw [hfun]; exact h
239	
240	variable {X S : Scheme.{0}}
241	
242	/-- **Serre finiteness in engine-ready form** (companion to
243	`sectionGradedModule_fg`; `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`, [Nitsure] §1): for
244	`π : X ⟶ S` projective carrying the very ample line bundle `L` and a coherent
245	(finitely presented) sheaf `F`, the graded Hilbert function
246	`m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` at each fibre over `s : S` is packaged by
247	a `GradedHilbert` datum over the residue field `κ(s)`.
248	
249	NAMED LEAF (`sorry`): this is `sectionGradedModule_fg` (Serre) restated in the
250	form the graded Hilbert–Serre rationality engine consumes.  Taking `M` to be the
251	fibre section module `⊕ₘ Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` and the `d+1` generators to be
252	the coordinate sections of the very-ample embedding `X_s ↪ ℙ^d`, its content is
253	the finite generation of that module over the homogeneous coordinate ring
254	`κ(s)[x₀,…,x_d]` — the `MvPolynomial` reshaping documented in inbox `I-0109`.
255	Compared with the abstract-section-ring finiteness of `sectionGradedModule_fg`,
256	this form additionally records the degree-one coordinate action, which is what
257	makes the Hilbert polynomial genuine
258	(`hilbertPolynomial_eval_eventually_of_projective`). -/
259	theorem gradedHilbert_fiber (π : X ⟶ S) (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)
260	    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
261	    Nonempty (GradedHilbert (S.residueField s) (Scheme.hilbertFunction π L F s)) :=
262	  sorry
263	
264	/-- **The graded Hilbert function of a projective morphism is a rational Hilbert
265	function**: from the engine-ready Serre finiteness `gradedHilbert_fiber` via
266	`GradedHilbert.isRatHilb`.  Inherits the single leaf `gradedHilbert_fiber` and no
267	other. -/
268	theorem hilbertFunction_isRatHilb (π : X ⟶ S) (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)
269	    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
270	    ∃ d : ℕ, IsRatHilb (fun m => (Scheme.hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)) d :=
271	  let ⟨H⟩ := gradedHilbert_fiber π L F s hproj hF
272	  ⟨H.r, H.isRatHilb⟩
273	
274	/-- **The Hilbert polynomial genuinely computes the Hilbert function** for a
275	projective morphism over a locally noetherian base: for all `m ≫ 0`,
276	`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial π L F s` evaluated at `m` equals
277	`dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` (`Scheme.hilbertFunction π L F s m`).
278	Combines `hilbertFunction_isRatHilb` with the polynomial-extraction API
279	`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eval_of_isRatHilb`; this is the point at which the
280	Hilbert polynomial ceases to be a formal `Classical.choice` and becomes the
281	Snapper/Hilbert polynomial of the fibre.  Inherits the single leaf
282	`gradedHilbert_fiber`. -/
283	theorem hilbertPolynomial_eval_eventually_of_projective [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
284	    (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)
285	    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
286	    ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
287	      (Scheme.hilbertPolynomial π L F s).eval (m : ℚ)
288	        = (Scheme.hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ) := by
289	  obtain ⟨d, hrat⟩ := hilbertFunction_isRatHilb π L F s hproj hF
290	  exact Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eval_of_isRatHilb π L F s hrat
291	
292	end AlgebraicGeometry
293

data.content:
1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.SerreTwist
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotFunctorDef
9	
10	/-!
11	# Projective morphisms carrying a very ample line bundle
12	
13	Mathlib v4.31 has no projective-morphism class and no (very) ampleness
14	vocabulary.  Following the encoding settled in inbox `I-0118` (comment
15	`C-0002`), this file defines:
16	
17	* `ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf n₀ S m` — the Serre twist `O(m)` on the
18	  relative projective space `ℙ(n₀; S)`, the pullback of
19	  `ProjTwist.serreTwist` from the integral model along `toProjInt`;
20	* `ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheafBaseChange` — `O(m)` commutes with base
21	  change of the ambient projective space;
22	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith π L` — the **projective-with-`L`** predicate:
23	  `π : X ⟶ S` factors through a closed immersion `i : X ↪ ℙ(Fin (d+1); S)`
24	  over `S` with `L ≅ i^* O(1)`;
25	
26	with the stability facts the Quot-scheme endgame consumes:
27	
28	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.isProper` — projective morphisms are proper;
29	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.comp_isClosedImmersion` — a closed immersion
30	  into a projective scheme is projective (carrying the restricted bundle);
31	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.baseChange` — stability under base change
32	  (carrying the pulled-back bundle).
33	
34	Everything is at `Scheme.{0}`: the Serre twist rests on the descent engine
35	`Scheme.Modules.glue`, which is universe-monomorphic at `Scheme.GlueData.{0}`
36	(`GlueDescent.lean:934`).
37	
38	Blueprint: `def:twisting_sheaf`, `def:projective_with`,
39	`lem:projective_with_proper`, `lem:projective_with_closed_immersion`,
40	`lem:projective_with_base_change`
41	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
42	-/
43	
44	open CategoryTheory Limits MvPolynomial
45	
46	noncomputable section
47	
48	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
49	
50	namespace ProjectiveSpace
51	
52	variable (n₀ : Type) (S : Scheme.{0})
53	
54	/-- The Serre twist `O(m)` on the relative projective space `ℙ(n₀; S)`:
55	the pullback of the glued twisting sheaf on the integral model. -/
56	def twistingSheaf (m : ℕ) : (ℙ(n₀; S)).Modules :=
57	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (toProjInt n₀ S)).obj (ProjTwist.serreTwist n₀ m)
58	
59	variable {S} {S' : Scheme.{0}}
60	
61	/-- The Serre twist commutes with base change of the ambient projective
62	space: `(map g)^* O(m) ≅ O(m)`. -/
63	def twistingSheafBaseChange (g : S' ⟶ S) (m : ℕ) :
64	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (map n₀ g)).obj (twistingSheaf n₀ S m) ≅
65	      twistingSheaf n₀ S' m :=
66	  Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso (map_toProjInt n₀ g) (ProjTwist.serreTwist n₀ m)
67	
68	end ProjectiveSpace
69	
70	/-- `π : X ⟶ S` is a **projective morphism carrying the line bundle `L`**
71	([Nitsure] §5, [EGA II] 5.5): there are a closed immersion
72	`i : X ↪ ℙ(Fin (d+1); S)` over `S` and an isomorphism of `L` with the
73	pullback of the Serre twist `O(1)`.  This is the faithful encoding of
74	"projective with relatively very ample `L`" settled in inbox `I-0118`. -/
75	def Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith {X S : Scheme.{0}} (π : X ⟶ S) (L : X.Modules) :
76	    Prop :=
77	  ∃ (d : ℕ) (i : X ⟶ ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S)),
78	    IsClosedImmersion i ∧ i ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S) = π ∧
79	      Nonempty (L ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback i).obj
80	        (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1))
81	
82	namespace Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith
83	
84	variable {X S : Scheme.{0}} {π : X ⟶ S} {L : X.Modules}
85	
86	/-- **Projective morphisms are proper**: a closed immersion is proper, the
87	structural morphism of projective space is proper, and properness is stable
88	under composition. -/
89	theorem isProper (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) : IsProper π := by
90	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, -⟩ := h
91	  haveI := hi
92	  rw [← hcomp]
93	  infer_instance
94	
95	/-- **Composition with a closed immersion**: if `π` is projective carrying
96	`L` and `j` is a closed immersion into `X`, then `j ≫ π` is projective
97	carrying `j^* L`. -/
98	theorem comp_isClosedImmersion (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) {Y : Scheme.{0}}
99	    (j : Y ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion j] :
100	    (j ≫ π).IsProjectiveWith ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj L) := by
101	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
102	  haveI := hi
103	  refine ⟨d, j ≫ i, inferInstance, by rw [Category.assoc, hcomp], ?_⟩
104	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫
105	    Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso (rfl : j ≫ i = j ≫ i)
106	      (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1)⟩
107	
108	/-- The comparison morphism from the base-changed total space into the
109	base-changed projective space. -/
110	private def baseChangeLift {S' : Scheme.{0}} (g : S' ⟶ S) {d : ℕ}
111	    (i : X ⟶ ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S)) (hcomp : i ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S) = π) :
112	    pullback π g ⟶ ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S') :=
113	  (ProjectiveSpace.isPullback_map (Fin (d + 1)) g).lift
114	    (pullback.fst π g ≫ i) (pullback.snd π g)
115	    (by rw [Category.assoc, hcomp, pullback.condition])
116	
117	/-- **Stability under base change**: if `π : X ⟶ S` is projective carrying
118	`L` and `g : S' ⟶ S`, then the base change `X ×_S S' ⟶ S'` is projective
119	carrying the pullback of `L`. -/
120	theorem baseChange (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) {S' : Scheme.{0}} (g : S' ⟶ S) :
121	    (pullback.snd π g).IsProjectiveWith
122	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π g)).obj L) := by
123	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
124	  haveI := hi
125	  refine ⟨d, baseChangeLift g i hcomp, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
126	  · -- the comparison square exhibits the lift as the base change of `i`
127	    have h1 : baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S') ↘ S')
128	        = pullback.snd π g := IsPullback.lift_snd _ _ _ _
129	    have hsq : IsPullback (baseChangeLift g i hcomp) (pullback.fst π g)
130	        (ProjectiveSpace.map (Fin (d + 1)) g) i := by
131	      have hbig : IsPullback
132	          (baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S') ↘ S'))
133	          (pullback.fst π g) g (i ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S)) := by
134	        rw [h1, hcomp]
135	        exact (IsPullback.of_hasPullback π g).flip
136	      exact IsPullback.of_right hbig
137	        (IsPullback.lift_fst _ _ _ _)
138	        (ProjectiveSpace.isPullback_map (Fin (d + 1)) g).flip
139	    exact MorphismProperty.of_isPullback hsq.flip hi
140	  · exact IsPullback.lift_snd _ _ _ _
141	  · refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π g)).mapIso e ≪≫
142	      Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso (IsPullback.lift_fst _ _ _ _ :
143	        baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ ProjectiveSpace.map (Fin (d + 1)) g
144	          = pullback.fst π g ≫ i).symm
145	        (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1) ≪≫ ?_⟩
146	    -- collapse `(lift ≫ map)^* O(1)` to `lift^* (map^* O(1))`, then use the
147	    -- base-change isomorphism of the twist
148	    exact (Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso
149	        (rfl : baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ ProjectiveSpace.map (Fin (d + 1)) g
150	          = _) (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1)).symm ≪≫
151	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (baseChangeLift g i hcomp)).mapIso
152	        (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheafBaseChange (Fin (d + 1)) g 1)
153	
154	end Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith
155	
156	end AlgebraicGeometry
157

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Relative projective space `ℙ(n; S)`
10	
11	Mathlib v4.31 has the absolute `Proj` of a ℕ-graded ring together with the
12	properness package for `Proj.toSpecZero` (`ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean`),
13	but no relative projective space over an arbitrary base scheme.  Following the
14	`AffineSpace` pattern (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineSpace.lean`) and the
15	encoding settled in inbox `I-0118` (comment `C-0002`), this file defines
16	
17	* `AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpace n S` (notation `ℙ(n; S)`): the relative
18	  projective space with homogeneous coordinates indexed by `n : Type u`, i.e.
19	  the pullback of the integral model
20	  `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n] = Proj (MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift ℤ))`
21	  along `S ⟶ ⊤_ Scheme`;
22	* the structural morphism `ℙ(n; S) ↘ S` (a `CanonicallyOver` instance) with
23	  `IsProper` (hence `IsSeparated`, `UniversallyClosed`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`,
24	  `QuasiCompact`) derived by base change from the Mathlib instances on
25	  `Proj.toSpecZero`;
26	* functoriality `ProjectiveSpace.map` in the base and the base-change square
27	  `ProjectiveSpace.isPullback_map`.
28	
29	The key observations making the transfer work: the degree-zero part of the
30	homogeneous coordinate ring is the copy of `ULift ℤ` given by the constants
31	(`MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule_zero`), so `Spec (𝒜 0)` is terminal and
32	`terminal.from (Proj 𝒜)` factors as `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` followed by an
33	isomorphism; and for finitely many variables the coordinate ring is of finite
34	type over its degree-zero part, which is exactly the hypothesis of the Mathlib
35	properness instances.
36	
37	Blueprint: `def:projective_space`, `lem:projective_space_proper`
38	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
39	-/
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits MvPolynomial
42	
43	noncomputable section
44	
45	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
46	
47	universe u
48	
49	/-- The total-degree grading on the homogeneous coordinate ring
50	`ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]` of the integral model of projective space (with `ULift ℤ`
51	coefficients for universe polymorphism, as in `AffineSpace`). -/
52	local notation3 "𝒫[" n "]" => homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift ℤ)
53	
54	/-- The total-degree grading is a graded ring structure.  Mathlib deliberately
55	does not register `MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` as a global instance (other
56	weightings of the same polynomial ring exist); for the fixed homogeneous
57	coordinate ring of projective space we register it project-wide. -/
58	instance (n : Type u) : GradedRing 𝒫[n] :=
59	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
60	
61	variable (n : Type u) (S : Scheme.{u})
62	
63	/-- `ℙ(n; S)` is the projective space over `S` with homogeneous coordinates
64	indexed by `n : Type u` (e.g. `ULift (Fin (m + 1))` for `ℙ^m_S`).  It is the
65	base change to `S` of the integral model `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]`. -/
66	def ProjectiveSpace (n : Type u) (S : Scheme.{u}) : Scheme.{u} :=
67	  pullback (terminal.from S)
68	    (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))))
69	
70	namespace ProjectiveSpace
71	
72	/-- `ℙ(n; S)` is the projective `n`-space over `S`. -/
73	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation "ℙ(" n "; " S ")" => ProjectiveSpace n S
74	
75	instance canonicallyOver : (ℙ(n; S)).CanonicallyOver S where
76	  hom := pullback.fst _ _
77	
78	lemma over_eq_fst : ℙ(n; S) ↘ S = pullback.fst _ _ := rfl
79	
80	/-- The projection from `ℙ(n; S)` to the integral model `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]`. -/
81	def toProjInt : ℙ(n; S) ⟶ Proj 𝒫[n] := pullback.snd _ _
82	
83	lemma toProjInt_eq_snd : toProjInt n S = pullback.snd _ _ := rfl
84	
85	@[reassoc]
86	lemma over_terminal_comp :
87	    (ℙ(n; S) ↘ S) ≫ terminal.from S = toProjInt n S ≫ terminal.from (Proj 𝒫[n]) :=
88	  pullback.condition
89	
90	section GradeZero
91	
92	/-- The degree-zero part of the homogeneous coordinate ring is the copy of
93	`ULift ℤ` given by the constants. -/
94	lemma bijective_algebraMap_gradeZero :
95	    Function.Bijective
96	      (algebraMap (ULift.{u} ℤ) (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)) := by
97	  constructor
98	  · intro a b hab
99	    have := congrArg Subtype.val hab
100	    simpa using MvPolynomial.C_injective n (ULift.{u} ℤ) this
101	  · intro x
102	    obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := Submodule.mem_one.mp
103	      ((MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule_zero (σ := n) (R := ULift.{u} ℤ)).le x.2)
104	    exact ⟨y, Subtype.ext (by simpa using hy)⟩
105	
106	/-- The degree-zero part of the homogeneous coordinate ring, as a ring, is
107	`ULift ℤ`. -/
108	def gradeZeroRingEquiv :
109	    ULift.{u} ℤ ≃+* homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0 :=
110	  RingEquiv.ofBijective
111	    (algebraMap (ULift.{u} ℤ) (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0))
112	    (bijective_algebraMap_gradeZero n)
113	
114	/-- `Spec` of the degree-zero part of the homogeneous coordinate ring is a
115	terminal scheme. -/
116	def specGradeZeroIsTerminal :
117	    IsTerminal (Spec (.of (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0))) :=
118	  specULiftZIsTerminal.{u}.ofIso
119	    (Scheme.Spec.mapIso (gradeZeroRingEquiv n).toCommRingCatIso.op).symm
120	
121	instance :
122	    IsIso (terminal.from (Spec (.of (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)))) :=
123	  isIso_of_isTerminal (specGradeZeroIsTerminal n) terminalIsTerminal _
124	
125	/-- For finitely many variables, the homogeneous coordinate ring is of finite
126	type over its degree-zero part — the hypothesis of the Mathlib properness
127	package for `Proj.toSpecZero`. -/
128	instance [Finite n] :
129	    Algebra.FiniteType (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)
130	      (MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) := by
131	  haveI := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (R := ULift.{u} ℤ)
132	    (S := homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)
133	    (A := MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) fun r => rfl
134	  exact Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType
135	    (R := ULift.{u} ℤ) (S := homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)
136	    (A := MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
137	
138	end GradeZero
139	
140	section Instances
141	
142	instance isProper_terminalFrom_proj [Finite n] :
143	    IsProper (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))) := by
144	  rw [← terminal.comp_from (Proj.toSpecZero (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))]
145	  infer_instance
146	
147	instance isSeparated_terminalFrom_proj :
148	    IsSeparated (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))) := by
149	  rw [← terminal.comp_from (Proj.toSpecZero (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))]
150	  infer_instance
151	
152	/-- The structural morphism of relative projective space is proper.  Its
153	parent classes (`IsSeparated`, `UniversallyClosed`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`,
154	hence also `QuasiCompact`) follow by instance projection. -/
155	instance isProper_over [Finite n] : IsProper (ℙ(n; S) ↘ S) :=
156	  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ (isProper_terminalFrom_proj n)
157	
158	instance isSeparated_over : IsSeparated (ℙ(n; S) ↘ S) :=
159	  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ (isSeparated_terminalFrom_proj n)
160	
161	end Instances
162	
163	section Functorial
164	
165	variable {S} {T : Scheme.{u}}
166	
167	/-- `ℙ(n; S)` is functorial in the base `S`. -/
168	def map (f : S ⟶ T) : ℙ(n; S) ⟶ ℙ(n; T) :=
169	  pullback.map _ _ _ _ f (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) (by simp) (by simp)
170	
171	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
172	lemma map_fst (f : S ⟶ T) :
173	    map n f ≫
174	        pullback.fst (terminal.from T)
175	          (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))) =
176	      pullback.fst (terminal.from S)
177	          (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))) ≫ f :=
178	  pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
179	
180	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
181	lemma map_snd (f : S ⟶ T) :
182	    map n f ≫
183	        pullback.snd (terminal.from T)
184	          (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))) =
185	      pullback.snd (terminal.from S)
186	        (terminal.from (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))) :=
187	  (pullback.lift_snd _ _ _).trans (Category.comp_id _)
188	
189	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
190	lemma map_over (f : S ⟶ T) : map n f ≫ ℙ(n; T) ↘ T = (ℙ(n; S) ↘ S) ≫ f := by
191	  rw [over_eq_fst, over_eq_fst]
192	  exact pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
193	
194	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
195	lemma map_toProjInt (f : S ⟶ T) : map n f ≫ toProjInt n T = toProjInt n S := by
196	  rw [toProjInt_eq_snd, toProjInt_eq_snd]
197	  exact (pullback.lift_snd _ _ _).trans (Category.comp_id _)
198	
199	@[simp]
200	lemma map_id : map n (𝟙 S) = 𝟙 ℙ(n; S) := by
201	  apply pullback.hom_ext
202	  · exact (map_fst n (𝟙 S)).trans
203	      ((Category.comp_id _).trans (Category.id_comp _).symm)
204	  · exact (map_snd n (𝟙 S)).trans (Category.id_comp _).symm
205	
206	@[reassoc, simp]
207	lemma map_comp {U : Scheme.{u}} (f : S ⟶ T) (g : T ⟶ U) :
208	    map n (f ≫ g) = map n f ≫ map n g := by
209	  apply pullback.hom_ext
210	  · exact (map_fst n (f ≫ g)).trans <| ((Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans <|
211	      (congrArg (· ≫ g) (map_fst n f).symm).trans <| (Category.assoc _ _ _).trans <|
212	        congrArg (map n f ≫ ·) (map_fst n g).symm).trans (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
213	  · exact (map_snd n (f ≫ g)).trans <| ((map_snd n f).symm.trans <|
214	      congrArg (map n f ≫ ·) (map_snd n g).symm).trans (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
215	
216	/-- Relative projective space commutes with base change: the square
217	
218	```
219	ℙ(n; S) ⟶ ℙ(n; T)
220	   |          |
221	   S    ⟶    T
222	```
223	
224	is a pullback. -/
225	lemma isPullback_map (f : S ⟶ T) :
226	    IsPullback (map n f) (ℙ(n; S) ↘ S) (ℙ(n; T) ↘ T) f := by
227	  have hbig : IsPullback (toProjInt n S) (ℙ(n; S) ↘ S)
228	      (terminal.from (Proj 𝒫[n])) (terminal.from S) :=
229	    (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (terminal.from S) (terminal.from (Proj 𝒫[n]))).flip
230	  have hright : IsPullback (toProjInt n T) (ℙ(n; T) ↘ T)
231	      (terminal.from (Proj 𝒫[n])) (terminal.from T) :=
232	    (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (terminal.from T) (terminal.from (Proj 𝒫[n]))).flip
233	  refine IsPullback.of_right ?_ (map_over n f) hright
234	  rw [map_toProjInt, terminal.comp_from f]
235	  exact hbig
236	
237	end Functorial
238	
239	end ProjectiveSpace
240	
241	end AlgebraicGeometry
242

130	
131	variable (n : Type u)
132	
133	/-- The variable `Xᵢ` is homogeneous of degree `1`. -/
134	lemma X_mem_deg_one (i : n) :
135	    (X i : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) ∈ homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 1 :=
136	  isHomogeneous_X _ _
137	
138	/-- The product `XᵢXⱼ` is homogeneous of degree `2`. -/
139	lemma X_mul_X_mem_deg_two (i j : n) :
140	    (X i * X j : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) ∈
141	      homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 2 :=
142	  SetLike.mul_mem_graded (X_mem_deg_one n i) (X_mem_deg_one n j)
143	
144	/-- The basic opens `D₊(Xᵢ)` cover `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]`: the variables
145	generate the coordinate ring over its degree-zero part. -/
146	lemma iSup_basicOpen_X_eq_top :
147	    ⨆ i, Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i) = ⊤ := by
148	  refine Proj.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top' _ _ (fun i => ⟨1, X_mem_deg_one n i⟩) ?_
149	  rw [eq_top_iff]
150	  rintro a -
151	  have ha : a ∈ Algebra.adjoin (ULift.{u} ℤ)
152	      (Set.range (X : n → MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) := by
153	    rw [MvPolynomial.adjoin_range_X]; trivial
154	  induction ha using Algebra.adjoin_induction with
155	  | mem x hx => exact Algebra.subset_adjoin hx
156	  | algebraMap r =>
157	      have hr : (algebraMap (ULift.{u} ℤ) (MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) r
158	          ∈ homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0 := by
159	        rw [MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
160	        exact isHomogeneous_C _ _
161	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem
162	        (Algebra.adjoin (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)
163	          (Set.range (X : n → MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))))
164	        (⟨_, hr⟩ : homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)
165	  | add x y _ _ hx hy => exact add_mem hx hy
166	  | mul x y _ _ hx hy => exact mul_mem hx hy
167	
168	/-- The open cover of the integral model by the basic opens `D₊(Xᵢ)`. -/
169	def basicOpenCover :
170	    (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))).OpenCover where
171	  I₀ := n
172	  X i := (Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i)).toScheme
173	  f i := (Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i)).ι
174	  mem₀ := by
175	    rw [Scheme.presieve₀_mem_precoverage_iff]
176	    refine ⟨fun x => ?_, inferInstance⟩
177	    have : x ∈ ⨆ i, Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i) := by
178	      rw [iSup_basicOpen_X_eq_top]; trivial
179	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp this
180	    exact ⟨i, ⟨x, hi⟩, rfl⟩
181	
182	/-- The glue datum of the basic-open cover of the integral model. -/
183	@[reducible] def glueData : Scheme.GlueData.{u} := (basicOpenCover n).gluedCover
184	
185	/-- The fraction `Xᵢ/Xⱼ = Xᵢ²/(XᵢXⱼ)` as an element of the degree-zero
186	localized ring `(ℤ[X]_{XᵢXⱼ})₀`. -/
187	def awayFraction (i j : n) :
188	    Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j) :=
189	  Away.mk _ (X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n i j) 1 (X i * X i)
190	    (by simpa using X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n i i)
191	
192	/-- The fraction `Xⱼ/Xᵢ = Xⱼ²/(XᵢXⱼ)` in the *same* localized ring
193	`(ℤ[X]_{XᵢXⱼ})₀` — the inverse of `awayFraction`. -/
194	def awayFractionInv (i j : n) :
195	    Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j) :=
196	  Away.mk _ (X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n i j) 1 (X j * X j)
197	    (by simpa using X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n j j)
198	
199	lemma awayFraction_mul_inv (i j : n) :
200	    awayFraction n i j * awayFractionInv n i j = 1 := by
201	  apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
202	  rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_mul, HomogeneousLocalization.val_one,
203	    awayFraction, awayFractionInv, Away.val_mk, Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_mul]
204	  have hnum : (X i * X i) * (X j * X j)
205	      = ((X i * X j) ^ 1 * (X i * X j) ^ 1 : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) := by
206	    ring
207	  rw [hnum]
208	  exact Localization.mk_self
209	    ((⟨(X i * X j) ^ 1, 1, rfl⟩ :
210	        Submonoid.powers (X i * X j : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) *
211	      (⟨(X i * X j) ^ 1, 1, rfl⟩ :
212	        Submonoid.powers (X i * X j : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))))
213	
214	/-- The fraction `Xᵢ/Xⱼ` as a unit of `(ℤ[X]_{XᵢXⱼ})₀`, with inverse
215	`Xⱼ/Xᵢ`. -/
216	def awayUnit (i j : n) :
217	    (Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j))ˣ where
218	  val := awayFraction n i j
219	  inv := awayFractionInv n i j
220	  val_inv := awayFraction_mul_inv n i j
221	  inv_val := (mul_comm _ _).trans (awayFraction_mul_inv n i j)
222	
223	/-! ## The transition units on the scheme-theoretic overlaps -/
224	
225	/-- The scheme-theoretic overlap of the glue datum factors through the basic
226	open `D₊(XᵢXⱼ) = D₊(Xᵢ) ⊓ D₊(Xⱼ)`. -/
227	lemma range_overlap_le (i j : n) :
228	    Set.range (pullback.fst ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ≫
229	        (basicOpenCover n).f i).base ⊆
230	      Set.range ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
231	        (X i * X j)).ι).base := by
232	  rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι, Proj.basicOpen_mul]
233	  rintro _ ⟨v, rfl⟩
234	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
235	  · have hx : ((pullback.fst _ _ ≫ (basicOpenCover n).f i) v : Proj _) ∈
236	        Set.range ⇑((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
237	          (X i)).ι) := by
238	      rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
239	      exact ⟨_, rfl⟩
240	    exact (Scheme.Opens.range_ι _) ▸ hx
241	  · have hcond := congrArg (fun (φ : pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i)
242	        ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ⟶ Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) =>
243	          φ.base v) pullback.condition
244	    simp only at hcond
245	    rw [hcond]
246	    have hx : ((pullback.snd _ _ ≫ (basicOpenCover n).f j) v : Proj _) ∈
247	        Set.range ⇑((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
248	          (X j)).ι) := by
249	      rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
250	      exact ⟨_, rfl⟩
251	    exact (Scheme.Opens.range_ι _) ▸ hx
252	
253	/-- The canonical morphism from the scheme-theoretic overlap of the `i`-th and
254	`j`-th charts to the basic open `D₊(XᵢXⱼ)`. -/
255	def overlapHom (i j : n) :
256	    pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ⟶
257	      (Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j)).toScheme :=
258	  IsOpenImmersion.lift
259	    ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j)).ι)
260	    (pullback.fst ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ≫
261	      (basicOpenCover n).f i)
262	    (range_overlap_le n i j)
263	
264	/-- The ring homomorphism carrying degree-zero fractions over `XᵢXⱼ` to
265	sections of the structure sheaf on the scheme-theoretic overlap. -/
266	def overlapRingHom (i j : n) :
267	    Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j) →+*
268	      Γ(pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j), ⊤) :=
269	  ((overlapHom n i j).appTop.hom.comp
270	    ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
271	      (X i * X j)).topIso.inv.hom)).comp
272	    (Proj.awayToSection (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j)).hom
273	
274	/-- The transition unit `(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)` as a unit of the sections of the structure
275	sheaf on the scheme-theoretic overlap of the `i`-th and `j`-th charts. -/
276	def overlapUnit (i j : n) :
277	    Γ(pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j), ⊤)ˣ :=
278	  Units.map (overlapRingHom n i j).toMonoidHom (awayUnit n i j)
279	
280	/-! ## Rank-one transitions -/
281	
282	/-- A unit of the global sections induces an automorphism of the structure
283	sheaf, multiplication by the unit (rank-one `matrixToFreeIso`). -/
284	def unitScalarIso {Y : Scheme.{u}} (v : Γ(Y, ⊤)ˣ) :
285	    SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf where

text:
The transition analysis settles the sign convention. Let me trace it precisely:

- `awayFraction n i j` = Xᵢ/Xⱼ, so `twistTransition n m i j` = multiplication by (Xᵢ/Xⱼ)ᵐ, going from the i‑frame pullback to the j‑frame pullback.
- In `Scheme.Modules.glue`, that descent datum sends the i‑frame section "1" to (Xᵢ/Xⱼ)ᵐ in the j‑frame. Classically, O(m)'s i‑trivialization has frame Xᵢᵐ, and Xᵢᵐ expressed in the j‑frame is Xᵢᵐ/Xⱼᵐ = (Xᵢ/Xⱼ)ᵐ. **These match** ⟹ `serreTwist n m` = O(m), not O(−m). A global section is a family fᵢ = s/Xᵢᵐ with fⱼ = fᵢ·(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)ᵐ, i.e. fᵢXᵢᵐ = fⱼXⱼᵐ = s ∈ Sₘ ⟹ Γ(ℙⁿ,O(1)) = linear forms (dim n+1). **The sign is correct.**

  rw [← hcomp]

input.new_string:
theorem isProper (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) : IsProper π := by
  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, -⟩ := h
  haveI := hi
  rw [← hcomp]
  infer_instance

/-- **Projective morphisms are locally of finite type**: immediate from
`isProper`, since properness extends `LocallyOfFiniteType`.  This lets the
Quot-scheme endgame and the Hilbert-polynomial existence theorem derive finite
type from projectivity instead of carrying it as a separate hypothesis. -/
theorem locallyOfFiniteType (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) : LocallyOfFiniteType π :=
  haveI := h.isProper; inferInstance

/-- **Projective morphisms are separated** (properness extends `IsSeparated`). -/
theorem isSeparated (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) : IsSeparated π :=
  haveI := h.isProper; inferInstance

/-- **Projective morphisms are universally closed** (properness extends
`UniversallyClosed`). -/
theorem universallyClosed (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) : UniversallyClosed π :=
  haveI := h.isProper; inferInstance

/-- **Transfer along an isomorphism of the carried bundle**: `IsProjectiveWith`
depends on `L` only up to isomorphism (it records the comparison `L ≅ i^* O(1)`
through `Nonempty`), so if `π` is projective carrying `L` and `L ≅ L'`, then `π`
is projective carrying `L'`.  Useful for consumers that only have the bundle up
to isomorphism. -/
theorem of_iso (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) {L' : X.Modules} (e : L ≅ L') :
    π.IsProjectiveWith L' := by
  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, ⟨eL⟩⟩ := h
  exact ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, ⟨e.symm ≪≫ eL⟩⟩

/-- **Composition with a closed immersion**: if `π` is projective carrying

input.new_string:
end Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith

namespace ProjectiveSpace

/-- **The structural morphism of relative projective space is itself
projective**, carrying the Serre twist `O(1)`: the canonical inhabitant of
`IsProjectiveWith`, with the identity closed immersion.  This exhibits
`ℙ(Fin (d+1); S) ↘ S` as the universal projective morphism and shows the
predicate is non-vacuous. -/
theorem isProjectiveWith_over (d : ℕ) (S : Scheme.{0}) :
    (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S).IsProjectiveWith (twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1) :=
  ⟨d, 𝟙 _, inferInstance, Category.id_comp _,
    ⟨((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).app _).symm⟩⟩

end ProjectiveSpace

end AlgebraicGeometry

\Cref{lem:serre_twist_transition_unit}, which satisfy the descent cocycle
  condition by \Cref{lem:serre_twist_cocycle}.
\end{definition}

\begin{remark}
[Sign convention]
  \label{rem:serre_twist_sign}
  The gluing datum realises \(\mathcal{O}(m)\), not \(\mathcal{O}(-m)\).  On
  \(D_+(X_i)\) the trivialisation identifies a local section \(s\) of the twist
  with \(s/X_i^m\); the nowhere-vanishing local frame is thus \(X_i^m\), and its
  expression in the \(j\)-frame is \(X_i^m/X_j^m = (X_i/X_j)^m\) — exactly the
  transition ``multiplication by \((X_i/X_j)^m\)'' of the definition.  Hence a
  global section is a compatible family \((f_i)\) with \(f_i = s/X_i^m\) and
  \(f_i X_i^m = f_j X_j^m =: s\) homogeneous of degree \(m\), so
  \(\Gamma(\operatorname{Proj}\mathbb{Z}[X_i], \mathcal{O}(m))\) is the group of
  degree-\(m\) homogeneous polynomials; in particular \(\mathcal{O}(1)\) has the
  \(X_i\) as sections and is very ample.  The special case \(m = 0\), where the
  factor \((X_i/X_j)^0 = 1\) makes the descent datum that of the structure sheaf,
  is recorded in Lean as \texttt{twistTransition\_zero}.
\end{remark}
